title: rubus montivagus
intro: Rubus montivagus, also known as the mountain raspberry or alpine bramble, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Rubus montivagus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and act as a natural diuretic. In herbal preparations, Rubus montivagus is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
